word_list.word: what you see is (what|all) you get
word_list.pronunciation: wňĆ Ćt juňź sňąiňź …™z wňĆ Ćt …Ēňź…Ļ ňą…Ēňźl juňź …°ňą…õt

senses.definition: used to imply that the external appearance of something or someone accurately reflects their true nature or qualities

examples.x: I prefer to work with people who are transparent and straightforward, because what you see is what you get, and there are no hidden agendas.

word_list.word: a straw will show which way the wind blows
word_list.pronunciation: …ź st…Ļňą…Ēňź w…™l  Éňąo ä wňĆ…™t É wňąe…™ √į…ô wňą…™nd blňąo äz

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of paying attention to small details or clues that can provide insights into larger situations or outcomes

examples.x: If you pay close attention to people's behavior and actions, you can often predict their intentions, as a straw will show which way the wind blows.

senses.definition: used to imply that by seeing things clearly and without illusions or biases, one can gain a deeper understanding of reality, emphasizing the importance of being observant and aware

examples.x: Real eyes realize that success is not measured by material possessions, but by the impact we have on others.

word_list.word: memory is the treasure of the mind

senses.definition: used to imply that the ability to remember and recall past experiences, knowledge, and emotions is a valuable asset that can provide insights, lessons, and personal growth

examples.x: As a historian, I know that memories are an invaluable source of information about the past; truly, memory is the treasure of the mind.

word_list.word: the longest journey is the journey within
word_list.pronunciation: √į…ô lňą…ĎňźŇč…°…ôst d íňą…úňźni …™z √į…ô d íňą…úňźni w…™√įňą…™n

senses.definition: used to emphasize that the most challenging and rewarding journey is the exploration of oneself, discovering one's thoughts, emotions, and true nature

examples.x: Many people spend their entire lives searching for meaning and purpose, but the truth is that the longest journey is the journey within.

senses.definition: used to imply that education and learning are powerful tools that can help individuals achieve success, make informed decisions, and make a positive impact on the world

examples.x: There is no doubt that knowledge is power, and those who seek to learn will always have an advantage in life.

word_list.pronunciation: …ź lňą…™…ĺ…ôl nňą…Ďňźl…™d í lňą…úňźn…™Ňč …™z …ź dňąe…™nd í…ö…Ļ…ôs őłňą…™Ňč

senses.definition: used to imply that having incomplete knowledge about something can lead to overestimating one's abilities or underestimating the complexity of the subject matter, which can be harmful

examples.x: After reading a few articles, Laura thought she was qualified to give financial advice but her lack of expertise soon became clear, exemplifying how a little knowledge is a dangerous thing without full understanding.

word_list.word: knowledge in youth is wisdom in age
word_list.pronunciation: nňą…Ďňźl…™d í …™n jňąuňźőł …™z wňą…™sd…ôm …™n ňąe…™d í

senses.definition: used to imply that gaining knowledge and education at an early age is valuable, as it can result in greater wisdom and understanding later in life

examples.x: As a young student, Sarah made it a priority to learn as much as she could, knowing that knowledge in youth is wisdom in age.

word_list.word: doubt is often the beginning of wisdom
word_list.pronunciation: dňąa ät …™z ňą…Ēf…ôn √į…ô b…™…°ňą…™n…™Ňč  Ćv wňą…™sd…ôm

senses.definition: used to imply that being skeptical or uncertain about something can lead to a deeper understanding and knowledge, as it encourages one to question and seek answers

examples.x: The student who is not afraid to ask questions and challenge assumptions is more likely to gain a deeper understanding of the subject matter, as doubt is often the beginning of wisdom.

word_list.word: a good mind possesses a kingdom

senses.definition: used to suggest that a good and intelligent mind is equivalent to possessing great power and influence

examples.x: The successful entrepreneur knows that his greatest asset is not his money or his business, but his own intellect; recognizing that a good mind possesses a kingdom.

senses.definition: used to suggest that intelligence, knowledge, and mental abilities are more valuable and useful than physical strength or brute force.

examples.x: The leader knew that in order to make informed decisions and lead effectively, better brain than brawn was essential - it was the ability to analyze data, think critically, and communicate effectively that set great leaders apart.

senses.definition: used to imply that identifying and understanding the root cause of a problem is a crucial step towards finding a solution or remedy

examples.x: The therapist reminded her client that a disease known is half cured, and encouraged him to explore the underlying causes of his anxiety in order to better manage it.

senses.definition: used for emphasizing the significance of intuition, perception, and sensory awareness

examples.x: When it comes to sensing danger, it's important to trust your instincts because, as they say, the nose knows.

senses.definition: used to suggest that seeking knowledge and wisdom can be a fulfilling experience that leads to better decision-making and a more meaningful life

senses.definition: used to imply that while excessive curiosity can have negative consequences, the pursuit of knowledge or exploration can also be rewarding and fulfilling despite the risks
